currentUse: Active Roman Catholic church — home of St. Casimir Parish (Švento Kazimiero Parapija), the Lithuanian successor parish of the Diocese of Cleveland, at 18022 Neff Rd, Cleveland, OH 44119
summary: The former Dievo Motinos Nuolatinės Pagalbos (Our Lady of Perpetual Help) church at 18022 Neff Road in Cleveland's Collinwood neighborhood is standing and remains a fully active Catholic church: it is the worship site of St. Casimir Parish, the successor parish created in October 2009 from the suppressed Our Lady of Perpetual Help and St. George Lithuanian parishes. The property was never sold — it stayed in diocesan hands and passed directly to the new parish, though True Lithuania reports the associated school and convent buildings (both 1962) have been rented out with most Lithuanian interior details removed. The parish is demonstrably alive as of July 2026: its own website posts a bulletin for July 11-12, 2026, lists Mass offerings through July 19, 2026, and announces an annual meeting of parishioners for July 29, 2026, with Masses on Saturday 5:00 PM (English), Sunday 8:30 AM (English) and 11:00 AM (Lithuanian), and 7:30 AM weekdays. Rev. Andrew M. Morkunas was appointed administrator effective April 30, 2025, an assignment he holds in addition to his chaplaincy at University Hospitals — a shared-clergy arrangement worth watching, though no diocesan restructuring threat to the parish was found. The church, hall and rectory carry a City of Cleveland designated-landmark listing under the name "Our Lady of Perpetual Help Church, Hall and Rectory (St. Casimir Parish)." A March 2022 Euclid Observer profile described the parish as thriving, with Lithuanian language classes, scout troops, dance groups, and an interior renovation then underway.

developments.headline: Successor parish St. Casimir opens in the former DMNP building
developments.detail: St. Casimir Parish opened October 25, 2009 as an ethnically diverse parish with a dual mission to unite and strengthen the Lithuanian community, formed from the suppressed Our Lady of Perpetual Help and St. George Lithuanian parishes; it worships at the former Our Lady of Perpetual Help site on Neff Road. Wikipedia's article on the Polish St. Casimir Church independently confirms the new parish was established in October 2009 at the suppressed OLPH site.

sources.title: St. Casimir Parish Thriving in Cleveland's Collinwood Neighborhood

developments.headline: Church, hall and rectory listed as a City of Cleveland designated landmark
developments.detail: The City of Cleveland lists 'Our Lady of Perpetual Help Church, Hall and Rectory (St. Casimir Parish)' at 18022 Neff Road among its designated landmarks; the city record gives a 1952 construction date and names Michael Boccia as architect. The designation ordinance date is not shown on the page.

sources.title: Our Lady of Perpetual Help Church, Hall and Rectory (St. Casimir Parish) — Designated Landmarks

developments.headline: Parish profiled as thriving; interior renovation underway
developments.detail: A Euclid Observer profile described the parish as thriving despite the pandemic, with three weekend Masses (Sunday 11 AM in Lithuanian), Lithuanian language classes, scout troops, dance groups, a Holy Name Society, a Ministry of Praise newsletter, and an interior church renovation scheduled for completion by Easter 2022.

sources.title: St. Casimir Parish Thriving in Cleveland's Collinwood Neighborhood

developments.headline: Rev. Andrew M. Morkunas appointed administrator of St. Casimir Parish
developments.detail: The Diocese of Cleveland announced (January 23, 2025) that Father Andrew M. Morkunas was appointed administrator of St. Casimir Parish (Neff Road), Cleveland, effective April 30, 2025, in addition to his assignment as Catholic chaplain of University Hospitals, moving into the parish rectory. The diocesan parish directory lists him as Administrator.

developments.headline: Parish active with current bulletin and full Mass schedule
developments.detail: The parish website posts a bulletin for July 11-12, 2026 and Mass offerings for July 11-19, 2026: Saturday 5:00 PM (English), Sunday 8:30 AM (English) and 11:00 AM (Lithuanian), weekday 7:30 AM (English), with First Friday and monthly Tuesday Eucharistic Adoration and Saturday 4:00 PM confessions. An annual meeting of parishioners was announced for July 29, 2026.

conflictsWithArchiveRecord: No contradictions. The archive record (closed Oct 18, 2009; merged with Šv. Jurgio into new Šv. Kazimiero worshiping in the former DMNP building) is confirmed by the Euclid Observer, Wikipedia, True Lithuania, and the Diocese of Cleveland directory (parish established 2009). Refinements: the successor St. Casimir Parish opened October 25, 2009 (one week after the archive's closure date, consistent). Minor discrepancy on founding year: the archive says 1929, while True Lithuania states the OLPH parish was established in 1928 — worth a cross-check against Draugas sources. Caution flag: Cleveland also has a separate, unrelated Polish St. Casimir parish (East 82nd St, reopened 2012); several web sources conflate the two, but every fact above was verified against the Neff Road (Lithuanian) parish specifically.
gaps: No sale ever occurred (property stayed diocesan), so no price/buyer applies to the church itself; however, True Lithuania's claim that the 1962 school and convent buildings are "rented out" could not be corroborated with tenant names, lease dates, or county property records — not searched in the Cuyahoga County auditor. The City of Cleveland landmark designation ordinance number and designation date were not findable (the detail page at planning.clevelandohio.gov returned 404); the city page also names architect Michael Boccia while True Lithuania credits Stasys Kudokas with the 1950-1952 design — unresolved conflict. The administrator of St. Casimir immediately before Fr. Morkunas's April 2025 appointment was not confirmed (a jbacevice@dioceseofcleveland.org contact appears on the parish site, suggesting Fr. Joseph Bacevice's involvement, but his role and any retirement were not verified). No press found on preservation campaigns, restructuring threats, or reunion events specific to this parish in 2023-2026.
